body:
Cleanup of the RTE solver layer. No numerical changes; every driver produces byte-identical fluxes; this is about halving the code where CPU/GPU asymmetries kept creeping in.

1. File & naming reorganization (zero logic): moved `RTE.jl` into `rte/`, split the monolithic `api.jl/api_types.jl/Optics.jl` into focused files, renamed solver fragments to `*_noscat/*_2stream`, and put the include list in architectural-layer order. Renames with one-release compat aliases: `Vmrs → VolumeMixingRatios`, `GrayUtils → GrayAtmosphere`, and `aerosol_idx() → aerosol_index_map()`.

2. Driver unification: the CPU and CUDA drivers now share device-agnostic per-(g-point, column) bodies (`*_gpt_col!`) with common helpers for cloud-mask building, aerosol masking, and flux accumulation, instead of ~2,100 lines of near-duplicate code. As part of this, the broadband shortwave direct beam `sw_direct_flux_dn` is now a true per-level profile (previously meaningful only at the surface row).

3. `LookupBundle`: `lookup_tables` returns a typed `LookupBundle` instead of a nested `NamedTuple`. Adds `save_lookup_tables/load_lookup_tables` for on-disk caching, so the spectral methods can run without `NCDatasets` once a cache exists (which will facilitate use of code in teaching).
title: CUDA kernel refactor/deduplication of CPU/GPU code (#3)
